title: Eucalyptus and mint room spray
subtitle: A refreshing eucalyptus and peppermint room mist using essential oils and witch hazel as a dispersant
excerpt: Essential oils do not disperse in water without a surfactant or alcohol-based dispersant. Witch hazel acts as a light solubiliser that keeps the essential oils suspended in the spray for long enough to mist evenly. This eucalyptus and peppermint blend gives a sharp, fresh room scent. Shake before each use.
sourceNotes: Witch hazel (Hamamelis virginiana) distillate as an alcohol-based dispersant for essential oils in room sprays: witch hazel contains 14 to 20% ethanol (depending on grade), which acts as a co-solvent. Standard room spray formulation reference. Eucalyptus EO (Eucalyptus globulus): IFRA standard for room sprays; no restriction at normal use levels. Peppermint EO (Mentha piperita): IFRA standard for room sprays; not for use directly on skin in high concentrations; this product is a room spray, not a skin spray.

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. A 100 ml bottle provides around 50 to 60 sprays.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Stable. Make a batch and use over several months.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Keeps 6 months in a sealed spray bottle in a cool dark place. Shake before each use. Discard if the oil and water phases permanently separate despite shaking, or if the scent changes.

glossaryTerms.definition: A substance that helps oil distribute evenly through water. In room sprays, witch hazel acts as a dispersant by partially solubilising the essential oils so the spray mists evenly rather than producing oily droplets.

content.text: Essential oils and water do not mix without help. A room spray made with water alone will separate immediately, leaving oil floating on the surface and clogging the spray nozzle. Witch hazel distillate contains enough ethanol to act as a

at these proportions, keeping the essential oils suspended long enough to spray evenly. Shake the bottle immediately before each use. This spray is for rooms, soft furnishings, and linen, not for direct skin application.

items.prepNote: 50% of blend; alcohol-containing grade, not alcohol-free

content.text: Add the essential oils to the witch hazel in the spray bottle first and swirl to mix the oils into the alcohol. Add the distilled water. Seal and shake well. The mixture may look slightly hazy, which is normal; it should disperse evenly when sprayed.

Shake before each use. Spray into the centre of the room from about 1 metre high, or onto soft furnishings such as cushion covers and curtains. Do not spray directly onto polished or lacquered wood surfaces. Do not spray near open flames.
Keep in a cool dark place. Keeps 6 months. Shake before each use. Discard if the spray becomes oily or the fragrance changes significantly.
